name: Dragon Tongue
image_alt: Yellow pods with purple streaks on a bush plant
description: A Dutch heirloom wax bean with stunning yellow pods streaked with purple. The most visually distinctive bean in the garden. Flavor is sweet and mild — one of the best-tasting bush beans available.
history: A Dutch heirloom, the 'Dragon's Tongue' name reflects the vivid purple streaking on yellow pods. Gained widespread popularity in American specialty and heirloom seed markets in the 1990s.
seasonal_quirks: The purple streaks fade completely when cooked — use raw or quickly blanched for visual impact. Very early to produce for a wax bean.
harvest_cues: Pods are ready at 5–7 inches — yellow with prominent purple streaks, plump but not bumpy with seed outlines. More forgiving of slightly oversize harvest than green beans.
variety_notes: Exceptional for fresh eating raw in salads. The color contrast with green salad vegetables is striking. Also good as a shell bean if allowed to mature.
preservation: Quick pickle in a light vinegar brine. Blanch and freeze — note color is lost.
